title: Lubia Polo (Green Bean Rice)
description: A deeply comforting one-pot Persian dish combining green beans, ground beef, and rice, all infused with a rich tomato base, cinnamon, and saffron.

instructions.text: Wash the basmati rice and soak it in salted water for 1 hour.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tbsp of oil and sauté the chopped onion until golden. Add the ground beef, turmeric, cinnamon, black pepper, and half the salt. Cook until meat is browned.

Stir the tomato paste into the meat mixture and cook for 2 minutes to deepen its flavor. Add the chopped green beans and 1/2 cup of water. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes until beans are tender. Set aside.
Boil a pot of water, drain the soaked rice, and parboil it for about 5-7 minutes. The grains should be firm in the center. Drain the rice.
In the large rice pot, add the remaining 2 tbsp of oil. Layer the parboiled rice and the green bean-beef mixture alternately, forming a pyramid.
Pour the bloomed saffron over the top of the rice.
Cover the pot with a towel-wrapped lid. Cook on medium-high for 5 minutes until steam builds, then lower heat to low and steam for 45 minutes.

tips: Frying the tomato paste slightly before adding the water removes its raw metallic taste and gives the dish a vibrant red color.
storage_notes: Keeps wonderfully in the fridge for up to 4 days. Reheats perfectly in the microwave or on the stovetop.
serve_with: Salad Shirazi, Mast-o-Khiar, or Torshi Liteh.

substitutions.substitute: diced lamb or beef stew meat
substitutions.note: Use small cubes of meat instead of ground meat, cooking longer in step 3 until tender.
Thaw and drain well before using to prevent a soggy mixture.
